# BCC+ Production Guide

## 1. Start with full captions

Always preserve an accurate closed-caption layer for accessibility. BCC/BCC+ is a creative semantic overlay, not a substitute for captions.

## 2. Extract the proposition

Ask:

> What is the actual claim or instruction?

Example transcript:

> Most people do not fail because they lack motivation. They fail because their day has no structure.

Proposition:

> Lack of structure, not lack of motivation, causes the failure.

## 3. Compress into semantic beats

Bad overlay:

> Most people do not fail because they lack motivation. They fail because their day has no structure.

Semantic compression:

> Not motivation.  
> Structure.

## 4. Apply BCC anchors

Use full bold for operator/control words:

- not
- no
- never
- stop
- start
- less
- more
- first
- then
- before
- after
- reduce
- shrink

Use partial bionic-style bolding only on selected concept words:

- **moti**vation
- **struc**ture
- **fric**tion
- **trig**ger
- **clar**ity
- **visi**bility

Example:

> **Not** **moti**vation.  
> **Struc**ture.

## 5. Place safely

Protect the main information source:

| Content type | Protect |
|---|---|
| Talking head | eyes and mouth |
| Product demo | hands and product |
| Fitness | joints and body line |
| Screen recording | cursor and UI target |
| Emotional story | face and eyes |
| Cooking/tutorial | hands, tool, and result |

## 6. Adapt density to visual load

| Visual load | Overlay rule |
|---|---|
| Low | 4–7 words allowed |
| Medium | 2–5 words |
| High | 1–3 words or label only |

## 7. Time to semantic beats

Recommended starting durations:

| Text type | Duration |
|---|---:|
| 1-word impact | 0.5–0.8 sec |
| 2–3 words | 0.8–1.2 sec |
| 4–7 words | 1.2–2.0 sec |
| 8+ words | Rewrite |

## 8. Run the muted test

Watch with no sound.

Pass condition:

> A viewer can understand the main idea or next action.

If it fails, add one clarifying word. Do not add a sentence.

## 9. Run the interference test

Ask:

> Does text block the face, mouth, product, hands, action, or UI?

If yes, move or shorten the overlay.

## 10. Publish rule

Only publish a BCC+ overlay if:

- full captions are also available,
- overlay has one idea per beat,
- emphasis is sparse,
- text does not cover critical visual information,
- muted viewing still communicates the gist.
